steps.title: Identify Text 1's key assumption
steps.content: Text 1 claims that graphic novels use language only “sparingly” and are “experienced as series of images”—positioning images as primary and language as secondary.

steps.title: Evaluate Text 2's counter-argument
steps.content: Text 2 contradicts Text 1 by arguing that language is essential—without text, illustrations are incomprehensible—and can be as literary as standard novels. The other choices mischaracterize Text 2's position by suggesting it concedes a flaw, redirects focus, or agrees with Text 1. That matches choice A — A is correct.
